title: MongoDB $bucket Operator - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Comprehensive guide on $bucket for categorizing values into ranges and buckets

bucket_overview.definition: $bucket categorizes documents into ranges (buckets) based on a field value
purpose: Group values into ranges (age groups, GPA ranges, score bands)
Categorize continuous values into discrete groups

section_1_beginner_concept.title: BEGINNER: What is $bucket?
section_1_beginner_concept.definition: Categorizes continuous values into discrete ranges (buckets)
section_1_beginner_concept.syntax: { $bucket: { groupBy: expression, boundaries: [min, ..., max], default: 'other', output: {...} } }
parameters: groupBy: field or expression to bucket by
boundaries: array of boundary values (must be sorted)
default: bucket for values outside ranges (optional)
output: aggregation operators for each bucket (optional)
section_1_beginner_concept.concept: Documents assigned to bucket based on value falling within range

section_2_beginner_simple.title: BEGINNER: Simple $bucket Examples
section_2_beginner_simple.important_note: _id shows bucket boundary (lower bound); default catches out-of-range values

section_3_beginner_with_output.title: BEGINNER: $bucket with Output Aggregations
section_3_beginner_with_output.syntax: { $bucket: { groupBy: expr, boundaries: [...], output: { count: {...}, avg: {...} } } }

beginner_examples.title: GPA buckets with average and count
Age buckets with names
beginner_examples.use_case: Rich bucket data with student names

intermediate_examples.title: Nested bucket analysis
Scholarship amount buckets

section_5_intermediate_bucketauto.title: INTERMEDIATE: $bucketAuto (Automatic Buckets)
section_5_intermediate_bucketauto.benefit: No need to manually define boundaries

intermediate_examples.title: Auto-bucket with output

section_5_intermediate_bucketauto.granularity_option: Can also specify granularity parameter for preferred bucket sizes

advanced_examples.title: Multi-stage with bucket and sort
Bucket after group
advanced_examples.use_case: Majors grouped by average GPA tier

advanced_examples.title: Conditional bucketing

memory_impact.scaling: Number of buckets = impact on memory

expression_complexity.avoid: Very complex expressions with multiple operations

practice_1_choose_boundaries.why: Fewer, meaningful buckets provide better insights

mistake_3_missing_lower_bound.problem: Values below first boundary go to default

mistake_5_wrong_default_type.solution: Use consistent data type for default

mistake_6_complex_expression_in_groupby.solution: Add calculation in $project first, then bucket

key_parameters: groupBy: field or expression to categorize
boundaries: sorted array of boundary values
key_concepts: Boundaries define bucket edges (must be sorted)
Use default to catch out-of-range values
Output can include count, avg, sum, arrays
summary.best_practice: Use 5-20 meaningful boundaries, include count in output, use default for edge cases
summary.progression: Beginner: Simple ranges → Intermediate: Multiple metrics, auto-bucket → Advanced: Complex pipelines and conditions
